title: NOC 21301 – Mechanical Engineers | GTR Immigration
description: Mechanical Engineers are in strong demand across Canada, driven by growth in manufacturing, energy, transportation, aerospace, and industrial-automation sectors. Classified under NOC 21301 (TEER 1), this occupation is well suited for Express Entry, Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs), LMIA-supported work permits, and long-term Permanent Residence (PR) strategies.

classification.category: 2 – Natural and applied sciences and related occupations
classification.teer: 1 – Occupations usually require a university degree

duties: Design machinery, tools, power plants, and systems
Prepare cost estimates and project schedules
Supervise installation and commissioning of systems
Prepare tender documents and evaluate bids
employment_requirements: Bachelor’s degree in mechanical engineering or related field
job_outlook: Mechanical Engineering remains immigration-relevant, especially for candidates with experience in:
additional_information: Canada’s industrial and energy sectors continue to recruit Mechanical Engineers for:
Manufacturing & processing plantsTransportation & automotive facilitiesAerospace systemsPower-generation infrastructureRobotics & automation
Typical annual salaries range between $50,900–$120,250 CAD, depending on province, specialization, and licensing status.
